title: Elevated Energy Prices Add to Fed’s Dilemma on Interest Rates
summary: Investors now expect that the Fed will delay a rate cut until September instead of July, as they had before the war in Iran began.

title: U.S. Eases Limits on Russian Energy as Oil Prices Soar
summary: The volatility in energy markets because of the war in Iran could not come at a better time for President Vladimir V. Putin.

title: ‘Nothing Will Remain of Tehran,’ Iranians Say Amid Heavy Bombing
summary: Some residents described the strikes overnight Monday into Tuesday as among the worst they had experienced since the war started.

title: Judge Weighs Trump Antisemitism Inquiry Seeking List of Jews From Penn
summary: The Trump administration has said it is investigating antisemitism. The government sued the University of Pennsylvania after it refused a request to provide information about Jewish faculty and staff.

title: D.C. Bar Begins Disciplinary Proceedings Against Ed Martin
summary: A new legal filing accused Mr. Martin, a senior Justice Department official, of an unethical pressure campaign against Georgetown University.

title: Kennedy to Undergo Rotator Cuff Surgery
summary: The secretary of health and human services, known for his Make America Healthy Again campaign, is expected to be back at work on Monday.

title: Mississippi and Georgia Elections: What to Watch in Today’s Primaries
summary: It’s Primary Day in Mississippi, where a younger Democrat is trying to oust a House veteran, and in Georgia, where Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former seat is up for grabs.

title: Can a Populist Democrat Flip New York City’s Only G.O.P. House Seat?
summary: Allison Ziogas, a first-time candidate, hopes to unseat Representative Nicole Malliotakis by appealing to the district’s union roots.

title: Democrats Sued to Find Out Whether Trump Will Send Armed Officers to Election Sites
summary: In a lawsuit filed Tuesday, the D.N.C. sought to compel the government to say whether it plans to deploy armed federal officers in this year’s elections.

title: Culture of Silence at Columbia Shielded Sexual Assault by Physician, Report Finds
summary: Two doctors affiliated with the university and NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital stepped down after an external report found that the administrators mishandled allegations of abuse against Robert Hadden.

title: San Francisco Bay Area Residents Weigh Possibility of BART Reductions
summary: The Bay Area Rapid Transit system was once so successful, it could rely mostly on riders to sustain itself. But the pandemic dealt BART an unusually heavy blow.

title: Noma Loses American Express and Blackbird Sponsors For Los Angeles Dinners
summary: Two companies have withdrawn from a series of dinners in Los Angeles after The New York Times reported allegations that René Redzepi attacked workers.

title: At Noma, Accusations of Past Physical Abuse
summary: Dozens of former employees say René Redzepi inflicted physical and psychological violence on the staff for years.

title: The Race to Stop Wildlife Trafficking in Africa
summary: In Nigeria, customs officers and conservationists are confronting the grim impacts of the $20 billion trade.

title: Michelle Pfeiffer Makes It Look Easy. It Isn’t.
summary: At 67, the actress is still reinventing herself, with two new TV shows, “The Madison” and “Margo’s Got Money Troubles,” and an evolving attitude about her work.

title: A.I. Incites a New Wave of Grieving Parents Fighting for Online Safety
summary: Blaming chatbots, they are joining an earlier push for better protections by parents who say social media contributed to their children’s deaths.

title: Who Are the Men Accused of Bringing Homemade Bombs to Gracie Mansion?
summary: A high school senior and a recent graduate, both from the Philadelphia suburbs, were charged by federal prosecutors with trying to support a terrorist group.

title: Gov. Kay Ivey Commutes Death Sentence of Charles ‘Sonny’ Burton
summary: The move by Gov. Kay Ivey, a Republican, is extremely rare. The inmate, Charles Burton, was scheduled to die even though the gunman’s sentence had been commuted years ago.

title: Review: A New ‘Tristan und Isolde’ at the Metropolitan Opera
summary: Yuval Sharon’s debut production featured an astonishing performance by the soprano Lise Davidsen. It is the event of the season.

title: I Asked a Former Trump Official to Justify This War
summary: Nadia Schadlow, a deputy national security adviser during Trump’s first term, explains how the U.S.-Israel war with Iran fits in with an “America first” agenda.

title: Trump Has No Idea How to End the War With Iran
summary: Only when the U.S.-Israeli bombing of Iran comes to a halt, without destroying the state, can the Iranian people sort out their fate.

title: War and the Economy
summary: We look at the conflict’s global economic impact.

title: Tell Us About Your Plans for a Longer Life
summary: If you are saving money and have a plan for the future, we want to hear how it’s going and any advice you have for others.

title: Dozens of States Weigh Continuing Live Nation Antitrust Case
summary: After the Justice Department reached a settlement with the concert giant, the judge overseeing the trial asked the states and the company to do the same.

title: USA Today Names Jamie Stockwell as Next Top Editor
summary: Ms. Stockwell, who recently left The Washington Post, is the third person named to the job in the past three years.

title: In Ancient Peru, a Parrot Trade That Crossed the Andes
summary: Scientists studied centuries-old bird feathers from an ancient tomb on the coast, and then traced the origins back to the Amazon.

title: The Killing Of Haiti’s President Heads to a U.S. Court. What to Know.
summary: Four men are accused in South Florida of plotting to kill President Jovenel Moise in 2021. His death plunged Haiti into chaos that continues today.
